message:
test(fcp-core): pin five fcp-core surface contracts via bead-driven test files

Why
---
Five beads asked for type-shaped contracts whose literal names do
not exist in the public fcp-core surface today. For each one, the
closest existing public type is what callers actually wire to, so
pinning the contract on that type is more honest than introducing
a new type alias just to satisfy the bead title:

- "ConnectorAttestation"      -> SupplyChainAttestation surface
- "OperationOutcome"          -> OperationStatus
- "PolicyEffect"              -> PolicyPreviewDelta (preview-delta tag)
- "PolicyVerdict"             -> DecisionReasonCode (carried by PolicyDecision)
- "ZoneJoinResponse"          -> EnrollmentStatus (enrollment vocabulary)

These five tests systematically lock the externally-visible serde
shape, Display, and (where applicable) round-trip semantics of
each surface so future drift produces a loud failure rather than
silently changing what operators see in audit records and what
downstream consumers (gateway, fwc, conformance harness) parse off
the wire. Same pattern as the earlier round of new test pins in
commits e91b89349, a930ddfb0, 86744dc9d.

What
----
- crates/fcp-core/tests/connector_attestation_display_serde.rs
  (new, 137 lines): pins SupplyChainAttestation Display + serde
  shape, including the SUPPLY_CHAIN_ATTESTATION_SIGNED_FIELDS
  ordering (which has to be byte-stable across releases because
  signatures are computed over its concatenation), the
  AttestationMaterial / AttestationMetadata / AttestationPredicateType
  variants, and the SUPPLY_CHAIN_ATTESTATION_FORMAT plus
  SUPPLY_CHAIN_ATTESTATION_SCHEMA_VERSION constants.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/operation_outcome_serde_roundtrip.rs
  (new, 73 lines): pins OperationStatus JSON+CBOR round-trip
  per variant and the wire form of the carried fields. Note in
  the file header explains why OperationStatus is the right
  surface for the "OperationOutcome" bead (no literal type
  exists; OperationStatus is the public operation-result
  classifier).

- crates/fcp-core/tests/policy_effect_display_serde_tag.rs
  (new, 114 lines): pins the PolicyPreviewDelta tag contract
  carried by PolicyPreviewEntry::delta. Notes that the type does
  NOT implement Display and that the stable identifier surface
  is the serde tag, so the tests assert the tag set rather than
  inventing a Display contract that does not exist.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/policy_verdict_display_serde_tag.rs
  (new, 95 lines): pins DecisionReasonCode Display + serde tag
  for every variant carried by PolicyDecision.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/zone_join_response_variant_matrix.rs
  (new, 109 lines): pins EnrollmentStatus as the public zone-join
  response vocabulary; per-variant JSON tag and round-trip,
  plus the documented meaning attached to each status.

Compatibility
-------------
Pure additions. No public API surface changes. The tests assert
the status quo of the wire form rather than introducing a new
contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
